sections.instruction: Questions 1-6: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 7-10: Complete the sentences below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

questions.content: The Family Welcome event in the art gallery begins at
The film that is now shown in the Family Welcome event is about
When do most of the free concerts take place?
Where will the 4 pm concert of Latin American music take place?
One of the boat race teams
options: A won a regional competition earlier this year
B has represented the region in a national competition
C has won several regional competitions

questions.content:
(7) Paxton Nature Reserve
Paxton is a good for seeing rare {{BLANK}} all year around.
(8) This is a particularly good time for seeing certain unusual {{BLANK}}.
(9) Visitors will be able to learn about {{BLANK}} and then collect some.
(10) Part of the {{BLANK}} has been made suitable for swimming.

sections.instruction: Questions 11-15: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 16-20: What is planned for each of the following facilities? Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter A-G next to questions 16-20.
sections.passage_or_context:
Changes in Barford over the last 50 years

Plans
A it will move to a new location
B it will have its opening hours extended
C it will be refurbished
D it will be used for a different purpose
E it will have its opening hours reduced
F it will have new management
G it will be expanded

questions.content: In Shona’s opinion why do fewer people use buses in Barford these days?
options: A the buses are old and uncomfortable
B fares have gone up too much
C there are not so many bus routes

questions.content: What change in the road network is known to have benefitted the town most?
options: A the construction of a bypass
B the development of cycle paths
C the banning of cars from certain streets

questions.content: What is the problem affecting shopping in the town centre?
options: C lack of restaurants and cafes

questions.content: What does Shona say about medical facilities in Barford?
options: B new medical practices are planned
C the number of dentists is too low

questions.content: The largest number of people are employed in
Match each facility with the planned change.
options: A it will move to a new location
B it will have its opening hours extended
D it will be used for a different purpose
E it will have its opening hours reduced
F it will have new management

sections.instruction: Questions 21-26: Complete the table below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer. Questions 27-30: Who is going to write each of the following parts of the report? Write the correct letter A-D next to questions 27-30.
sections.passage_or_context:
A Helen only
B Jeremy only
C both Helen and Jeremy
D neither Helen nor Jeremy

questions.content: Who is going to write each part of the report?
options: 28. how they had ideas for their stories

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
Ethnography in Business

Ethnography: research which explores human cultures

It can be used in business:
• To investigate customer needs and {{BLANK}}
• To help companies develop new designs

Examples of ethnographic research in business
Kitchen equipment
• Researchers found that cooks could not easily see the {{BLANK}} in measuring cups

Cell phone
• In Uganda, customers paid to use the cell phones of entrepreneurs.
• These customers wanted to check the {{BLANK}} used.

Computer companies
• There was a need to develop {{BLANK}} to improve communication between system administrators and colleagues.

Hospitals
• Nurses needed to access information about {{BLANK}} in different parts of the hospital

Airlines
• Respondents recorded information about their {{BLANK}} while travelling.

Principles of ethnographic research in business
• The researched does not start off with a hypothesis
• Participants may be selected by criteria such as age, {{BLANK}} or product used
• The participants must feel {{BLANK}} about taking part in the research.
• There is usually direct {{BLANK}} of the participants
• The interview is guided by the participant
• A lot of time is needed for the {{BLANK}} of the data.
• Researchers look for a meaningful pattern in the data.

questions.content:
(31) To investigate customer needs and {{BLANK}}.
(32) Researchers found that cooks could not easily see the {{BLANK}} in measuring cups.
(33) These customers wanted to check the {{BLANK}} used.
(34) There was a need to develop {{BLANK}} to improve communication between system administrators and colleagues.
(35) Nurses needed to access information about {{BLANK}} in different parts of the hospital.
(36) Respondents recorded information about their {{BLANK}} while travelling.
(37) Participants may be selected by criteria such as age, {{BLANK}} or product used.
(38) The participants must feel {{BLANK}} about taking part in the research.
(39) There is usually direct {{BLANK}} of the participants.
(40) A lot of time is needed for the {{BLANK}} of the data.
